summary.rationale: The implementation preserves the restrained Songs list, makes musical form immediately visible, and lets the three-part notation remain the dominant subject-specific surface at desktop and mobile widths.

notes: Existing empty, loading, error, ready, and not-found states remain defined.
There are no destructive actions or competing calls to action.
No new global theme token or custom semantic CSS class was introduced.
The new list row matches sibling information hierarchy and remains scannable.
The form digest improves orientation without repeating labels around each system.
Desktop and 390px layouts fit available width with no horizontal document overflow.
Music notation, rehearsal labels, chords, and staves drive the visual hierarchy.
The result avoids cards, badges, gradients, shadows, and marketing composition.
Geist title and metadata hierarchy remain consistent with the current product shell.
The detail page behaves as a readable musical instrument rather than an administrative record.
The compact form line is subject-grounded and useful for navigation.
No raw-token drift, density blindness, visual-noise stack, or music-surface-as-admin-ui pattern appears.
